summary: Claude Code CLI v2.1.170 (verified locally, mid-2026) has a mature programmatic surface: `claude -p` headless mode with text/json/stream-json output, `--json-schema` structured output, session resume via `--resume <uuid>`/`--continue`/`--session-id`, model aliases (fable/opus/sonnet/haiku; opus→Opus 4.8, sonnet→Sonnet 4.6 on the Anthropic API) plus `--effort low|medium|high|xhigh|max`, non-interactive permissions via `--permission-mode`/`--allowedTools`/`--dangerously-skip-permissions`, and cloud execution via `claude --remote` (claude.ai/code) with `--teleport` to pull sessions back. Live monitoring is available via stream-json events, `--include-hook-events`, hooks (Stop/StopFailure/Notification, including `type:"http"` handlers POSTing to an orchestrator), and per-session JSONL transcripts under `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-slug>/<session-id>.jsonl`. Facts verified by running `claude --help`, `claude agents --help`, a real `claude -p ... --output-format json` call, and fetching code.claude.com/docs (headless, model-config, hooks, claude-code-on-the-web pages). Note: starting June 15, 2026, `claude -p`/Agent SDK usage on subscription plans draws from a separate monthly Agent SDK credit.
headless_invocation:
Core: `claude -p "<prompt>"` (alias `--print`) runs non-interactively and exits. All CLI flags work with `-p`.

Output formats (`--output-format`, only with `--print`):
- `text` (default)
- `json` — single result object. Verified real shape: `{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"num_turns":1,"result":"ok","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"7b166a0f-...","total_cost_usd":0.0185,"duration_ms":...,"usage":{...},"modelUsage":{"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001":{...,"costUSD":...}},"permission_denials":[],"terminal_reason":"completed","uuid":"..."}`. Extract with jq: `claude -p "..." --output-format json | jq -r '.result'` and `.session_id`.
- `stream-json` — newline-delimited JSON events in real time. For token-level streaming add `--verbose --include-partial-messages`:
  `claude -p "Explain recursion" --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages`
  Filter text deltas: `... | jq -rj 'select(.type == "stream_event" and .event.delta.type? == "text_delta") | .event.delta.text'`

Schema-validated output: `claude -p "Extract function names" --output-format json --json-schema '{"type":"object","properties":{"functions":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}}},"required":["functions"]}'` → result in `.structured_output`.

Stdin piping: `cat build-error.txt | claude -p 'explain the root cause' > out.txt` (piped stdin capped at 10MB since v2.1.128; exceeding it exits non-zero). Streaming input: `--input-format stream-json` for realtime streaming input; pair with `--replay-user-messages` to get user messages echoed back for acknowledgment (requires stream-json in and out).

Recommended for orchestrators: `--bare` (skips hooks, plugins, MCP autodiscovery, CLAUDE.md, keychain — deterministic across machines; docs say it will become the default for `-p`). Pass context explicitly via `--append-system-prompt[-file]`, `--system-prompt`, `--settings <file-or-json>`, `--mcp-config`, `--agents <json>`, `--add-dir`, `--tools "Bash,Edit,Read"`. Cost cap: `--max-budget-usd <amount>` (print-only). `--no-session-persistence` for throwaway runs. Skills work in -p: include `/skill-name` in the prompt string.
status_monitoring:
1) stream-json events (primary mechanism): first event is `system/init` (model, tools, mcp_servers, `plugins` array, `plugin_errors` — use to fail CI when a plugin didn't load), then `assistant`/`user` message events per turn, `stream_event` deltas (with `--include-partial-messages`), `system/api_retry` on retryable API errors (`attempt`, `max_retries`, `retry_delay_ms`, `error_status`, `error` category like rate_limit/overloaded/billing_error), `system/plugin_install` (when CLAUDE_CODE_SYNC_PLUGIN_INSTALL set), and a final `result` event with `is_error`, `total_cost_usd`, `usage`, `modelUsage`, `permission_denials`, `terminal_reason`. Add `--include-hook-events` to also stream all hook lifecycle events (stream-json only).

2) Hooks (configured in settings.json or passed via `--settings`): events include Stop (turn finished), StopFailure (turn ended on API error, has `error_type`), Notification (`notification_type`: permission_prompt, idle_prompt, auth_success, etc. — fires when Claude is blocked waiting on permission), PreToolUse/PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure, SubagentStart/SubagentStop, SessionStart/SessionEnd, TaskCreated/TaskCompleted. Handler types: `command` (JSON on stdin), `http` (POSTs the JSON to a URL — ideal for an orchestrator webhook), `mcp_tool`, `prompt`, `agent`. Every hook payload includes `session_id`, `transcript_path`, `cwd`, `permission_mode`, `hook_event_name`.

3) Transcript files: full conversation persisted as JSONL at `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-path-with-slashes-as-dashes>/<session-id>.jsonl` (verified locally, e.g. `~/.claude/projects/-Users-void-Developer-personal-friday-kanban/81e0287d-....jsonl`). Tail this file for progress without touching stdout.

4) Token/cost tracking: `result` event / json output carries `total_cost_usd`, `usage` (input/output/cache tokens), and per-model `modelUsage` with `costUSD` — track spend per invocation.

5) Background/local agents: `claude agents --json` prints active background sessions as a JSON array without requiring a TTY (`--all` includes completed). Cloud sessions: `/tasks` inside the CLI, or claude.ai/code / mobile app.
session_management:
- Capture the session id from `--output-format json` (`.session_id`) or the `system/init` stream event, then send review feedback into the same session: `session_id=$(claude -p "Start a review" --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id'); claude -p "Address this review feedback: ..." --resume "$session_id" --output-format json`. Resumed runs keep full context (and keep the model the transcript was saved with).
- `-c` / `--continue` resumes the most recent conversation **in the current directory** — convenient but racy if you run multiple jobs in the same cwd; prefer explicit `--resume <uuid>` for orchestration.
- `--session-id <uuid>` pre-assigns a session ID at launch (must be a valid UUID) so the orchestrator can know the ID before the run starts.
- `--fork-session` with `--resume`/`--continue` creates a new session ID instead of mutating the original — useful for branching (e.g. trying two fix strategies from one review session).
- `--no-session-persistence` (print-only) disables saving; such sessions cannot be resumed.
- `-r/--resume` with no value opens an interactive picker (TTY only) — always pass the ID in scripts. `--from-pr <number|url>` resumes a session linked to a PR.
- Live injection into a running process: keep one `claude -p --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json` child alive and write user messages as JSON lines to stdin (this is what the Agent SDK does under the hood).
model_and_effort_selection:
- `--model <alias|full-name>` per invocation; `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` env var; `model` field in settings (precedence: /model > --model > env > settings).
- Aliases (verified from docs, mid-2026): `fable` (Claude Fable 5, `claude-fable-5` — most capable, not default anywhere), `best` (Fable 5 if org has access, else latest Opus), `opus` (→ Opus 4.8 / `claude-opus-4-8` on the Anthropic API; 4.7 on Claude Platform on AWS; 4.6 on Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry), `sonnet` (→ Sonnet 4.6), `haiku` (resolved to `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` in my verified run), `opus[1m]`/`sonnet[1m]` (1M context), `opusplan` (Opus in plan mode, Sonnet for execution), `default`. Pin versions with full names or `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_FABLE_MODEL`.
- Effort (adaptive reasoning): `--effort <low|medium|high|xhigh|max>` per session, or `CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL` env (takes precedence over everything), or `effortLevel` in settings (max not accepted there). Supported levels: Fable 5 and Opus 4.8/4.7: low–max; Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6: low, medium, high, max. Default `high` (Opus 4.7 defaults to `xhigh`). Unsupported level falls back to highest supported one below it. Per-skill/subagent override via `effort` frontmatter.
- Thinking: effort is the primary control on adaptive-reasoning models (Opus 4.7+, Fable 5; cannot be turned off on Fable 5). `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0` disables thinking on older models; `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING=1` reverts Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6 to fixed budgets. One-off deep reasoning: include the keyword `ultrathink` in the prompt. `ultracode` is a session-only setting (via `--settings '{"ultracode": true}'`) that sends xhigh and orchestrates dynamic workflows.
- Resilience: `--fallback-model sonnet,haiku` (comma-separated chain, max 3, --print only as a flag; `fallbackModel` array in settings persists it) auto-switches when the primary is overloaded/unavailable.
permissions_automation:
- `--permission-mode <mode>`, choices verified: `default`, `acceptEdits` (auto-approve file writes + mkdir/touch/mv/cp), `auto`, `dontAsk` (deny anything not in permissions.allow or the read-only set — good for locked-down CI), `plan` (read-only exploration), `bypassPermissions`.
- `--dangerously-skip-permissions` bypasses all checks ("recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access"); `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions` makes bypass available without defaulting to it.
- `--allowedTools` / `--disallowedTools` with permission-rule syntax: `claude -p "Run the test suite and fix failures" --allowedTools "Bash,Read,Edit"` or scoped: `--allowedTools "Bash(git diff *),Bash(git commit *)"` (the space before `*` matters: `Bash(git diff*)` would also match `git diff-index`).
- `--tools "Bash,Edit,Read"` restricts the built-in tool set itself ("" disables all); `--settings <file-or-json>` can inject full permissions.allow/deny rules; `--setting-sources user,project,local` controls which settings files load; `--strict-mcp-config` ignores all MCP config except `--mcp-config`.
- Critical for headless: in `-p` mode there is no permission prompt — a tool call not covered by allow rules/mode causes the run to abort (denials appear in `permission_denials` in the result JSON). Always pre-approve what the task needs.
remote_capability:
Cloud agents = Claude Code on the web (claude.ai/code, research preview for Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise; requires claude.ai subscription auth, GitHub connected via /web-setup or the Claude GitHub App).

- Launch from CLI: `claude --remote "Fix the authentication bug in src/auth/login.ts"` creates a new cloud session on Anthropic-managed VMs, cloning the cwd's GitHub remote at the current branch (push local commits first). Each `--remote` invocation is an independent parallel session. Repos without GitHub are auto-bundled and uploaded (force with `CCR_FORCE_BUNDLE=1`; <100MB; untracked files excluded). Default cloud environment selected with `/remote-env`.
- Scriptability caveat (verified by running it): `--remote` requires an interactive terminal. Actual error when stdout is piped: "Error: --cloud requires an interactive terminal. Non-interactive invocations (piped stdout, --init-only, --sdk-url) run locally and would silently ignore --cloud." So you cannot fire cloud tasks from a plain non-TTY child process — an orchestrator must allocate a PTY (e.g. node-pty), or use scheduled routines / the claude.ai surface instead.
- Track: `/tasks` inside any CLI session lists cloud/background sessions (press `t` to teleport into one); or watch on claude.ai/code / the Claude mobile app. Inside the cloud session, `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` (cse_ prefix) yields a transcript URL: `https://claude.ai/code/${CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID/#cse_/session_}` — have the agent embed it in PR bodies for traceability.
- Fetch results: cloud sessions push branches/PRs to GitHub (primary result channel); or pull the session into the terminal: `claude --teleport <session-id>` (or `claude --teleport` for a picker, `/teleport` in-session) — fetches/checks out the session's branch and loads full conversation history. Requirements: clean git state, same repo (not a fork), branch pushed, same claude.ai account. Handoff is one-way from CLI (web→terminal only; the Desktop app can push local→web). `--teleport` is distinct from `--resume` (local history only).
- Related: `claude ultrareview [PR-number|base-branch]` runs a cloud-hosted multi-agent code review and prints findings to stdout (this one is CLI-output friendly); `--remote-control [name]` is the inverse feature — exposes a local interactive session for monitoring from web/mobile; routines (scheduled cloud agents) run on cron. Cloud limits: fresh VM per session, ~5-min setup scripts with filesystem snapshot caching (~7-day expiry), network allowlists, GitHub via proxy, ZDR orgs excluded.
gotchas:
1) TTY: `-p` works fine without a TTY (Node child_process.spawn with piped stdio is supported), but `--remote` hard-requires an interactive terminal, and `--resume`/`--teleport` without an ID open TTY pickers. The workspace trust dialog is skipped whenever stdout is not a TTY — only run in trusted directories. Settings files that fail validation are SILENTLY ignored in -p mode.
2) Auth: `--bare` never reads OAuth/keychain — it strictly needs `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or an apiKeyHelper via `--settings` (verified: --bare with subscription-only auth returns `{"type":"result","is_error":true,"result":"Not logged in · Please run /login"}` — note it still exits with a parseable result JSON, so check `.is_error`, not just exit code). For subscription auth in CI use `claude setup-token` (long-lived token). From June 15, 2026, `-p`/SDK usage on subscriptions draws from a separate monthly Agent SDK credit.
3) Permission deadlock: headless runs can't prompt; un-allowed tool calls abort the turn. Pre-approve via --allowedTools/--permission-mode, and watch `permission_denials` in the result.
4) cwd: session transcripts, `--continue`, and project settings are all keyed to the working directory. Set `cwd` explicitly when spawning; `--continue` in a shared directory races across concurrent jobs — use explicit `--resume <uuid>` or pre-set `--session-id <uuid>`.
5) Concurrency: parallel `claude -p` runs in the same repo can clobber each other's files — use `git worktree` (or `-w/--worktree`) per job. `--session-id` must be a unique unused UUID.
6) Streaming: `-p --output-format stream-json` requires `--verbose` for the full event stream, and `--include-partial-messages` for token deltas; events are NDJSON on stdout — use a line-buffered reader (don't JSON.parse the whole buffer).
7) Startup variance: without `--bare`, a headless run loads the user's hooks, plugins, MCP servers, and CLAUDE.md — results differ across machines and startup is slower. Use `--bare` + explicit flags for reproducibility (it will become the -p default).
8) Background Bash tasks started by Claude are killed ~5s after the final result (since v2.1.163) — don't rely on a headless run to leave a dev server running.
9) Piped stdin capped at 10MB (exits non-zero); pass big inputs as file paths in the prompt instead.
10) Cost/runaway control: use `--max-budget-usd`, `--fallback-model` (both print-only), and parse `total_cost_usd`/`modelUsage` per run. Fable 5 requests can be safety-classifier-rerouted to Opus 4.8; in non-interactive mode a flagged request ends the turn with a refusal instead of falling back interactively.
11) Output buffering from Node: spawn with `stdio: ['pipe','pipe','pipe']`, write the prompt then end stdin if piping; quote prompts carefully or pass via stdin to avoid shell-escaping bugs (prefer execFile/spawn arg arrays over shell strings).
